codes_manifest.display: Traumatic ischaemia of muscle (traumatic compartment syndrome)
Fracture of shaft of tibia, closed
Acute arterial occlusion / acute limb ischaemia

attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 51 AUC answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AUC casebundles in this batch (n=51)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-MSK-04-00025 — ingest --reseq will assign the real sequence within the SPEC-ORTHO-04 bucket; SEQ is provisional (derived from source AUC-025).

Specialty tagged MSK (primary) + EMG — this is an orthopaedic surgical emergency, but the schema specialty enum has no ORTHO value, so MSK is used; case_id partition key is SPEC-MSK-04-00025. Maintainer may reclassify.

Difficulty tier set to atypical_presentation_high_risk: presentation is classic FOR compartment syndrome but high-risk and easily under-triaged as ordinary fracture pain (anchoring / pulse-present reassurance). Maintainer to confirm.

Age not stated in source — inferred adult (age 30) from prescribed oxycodone and 70 kg body weight despite 'patient and parent' present; please confirm on clinician review. If the patient is under 18, apply paediatric review and note no paediatric dose is authored here.
Sex at birth not stated in source — inferred male with no strong signal; please confirm.
Geographic context inferred RA1_major_city from access to a casting clinic, 000 ambulance and an ED with orthopaedic capability — please confirm.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who palpated the compartment, measured capillary refill (~4 s), gave morphine 5 mg SC and called 000. Reprojected to AI-appropriate action (recognise + immediate 000 + carer-deliverable first aid). Clinician exam findings (capillary refill, pulses, paresis grading, vitals, intracompartmental pressure) sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits.
Presentation layer de-anchored: escalating disproportionate pain and lay descriptions used, no diagnosis label in 00/01/02.
No dose synthesised: the only dose recorded (morphine 5 mg SC) is the in-person clinician's action from the source, marked not a telehealth action; no AI-prescribed dose authored.
All codes are candidates (unverified_pending_terminology_receipt); SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT to be confirmed against the terminology server.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
